name: Dark Technical
description: A dark-surface style tuned for developer and engineering tools: low-luminance backgrounds, precise accent colours for syntax and status, monospace accents, fine borders, and carefully managed contrast to reduce eye strain during long focused sessions.
emotional_impression: Focused, technical, precise, and built for deep work in low-light environments.
visual_principles: Dark neutral surfaces (not pure black) with layered elevation via subtle lightness steps
Restrained, saturated accents reserved for syntax, status, and primary actions
Monospace type for code, identifiers, and metrics
Fine 1px borders and subtle separators instead of heavy shadow
Carefully tuned non-pure-white foreground to limit halation
accessibility_risks: Pure white on pure black causes halation and discomfort for astigmatism and some low-vision users
Saturated accents on dark backgrounds can fail contrast or vibrate
Dark mode is not universally easier; some users with astigmatism read light mode better
performance_risks: Negligible, though OLED power savings are a minor benefit
appropriate_contexts: Developer and engineering tools used for long sessions
Products where a light-mode counterpart is also offered for choice
inappropriate_contexts: Long-form reading where dark backgrounds slow some readers
Audiences with high rates of astigmatism without a light-mode option
Marketing surfaces needing bright, energetic warmth
evidence: VS Code, GitHub dark, and Linear demonstrate mature dark-technical systems
Nielsen Norman Group, 'Dark Mode vs. Light Mode' finds light mode often yields better performance for users with normal vision, so dark mode should be a choice not a default
